title: Proteomic investigation of the effect of PDE4 inhibitor Crisaborole on atopic dermatitis

investigators.collaborator: Dr Ali Azimi and Professor Pablo Fernández-Peñas

sample_info.sample_type: tape-strip skin biopsies (treated AD, untreated AD, normal skin) across baseline and post-treatment timepoints from 15 patients

analytical_questions.primary_question: What proteomic changes does the topical PDE4 inhibitor crisaborole induce in treated atopic dermatitis lesions, and how do they compare with the corticosteroid betamethasone?
other_questions: Which proteins change in treated AD lesions (DAY45 vs DAY1) under crisaborole, and under betamethasone?
Does the crisaborole versus betamethasone treatment effect differ in the treated-AD proteome (interaction contrast)?
Which biological pathways are altered by each treatment, and which are shared versus treatment-specific?
Are the differential-abundance results robust to outlier sample exclusion (CRIS05-DAY45-TAD, BETA16-DAY45-TAD), prior systemic treatment, and MNAR-aware modelling?
Do the headline findings align with published drug-mechanism and atopic dermatitis literature?

qc.qc_question: Is the cohort balanced and complete across the design?
qc.qc_method: per-patient completeness checks; 15 patients x 6 samples (90 analysis + 16 pooled QC injections)

qc.qc_question: Does missingness indicate sample- or feature-level quality concerns?
qc.qc_method: MNAR diagnostic (per-protein mean log2 vs % missing); per-sample missingness 10-77%, median ~45%, tissue-driven MNAR

qc.qc_question: Are sample intensity distributions aligned across the run?
qc.qc_method: per-sample log2 intensity boxplots/densities; Spectronaut normalisation judged sufficient, no extra global normalisation

qc.qc_question: Is instrument quantification reproducible and is there run-order drift?
qc.qc_method: pooled-QC %CV (median 17.3%) and median log2 vs injection index; small significant drift not structured in analysis samples
PCA on complete-case proteins; CRIS05-DAY45-TAD and BETA16-DAY45-TAD flagged on PC2 (>70% missing / right-shifted intensity)

qc.qc_question: Should KNN imputation be the primary modelling input?
qc.qc_method: KNN vs NA-coded PCA/UMAP sensitivity; NA-coded matrix retained (KNN does not rescue outliers and adds a UMAP artefact)
